title: Sarga 93
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge. The passage moves from “While that supremely wonderful sacrifice was thus going on, Valmiki, the foremost among sages, quickly arrived with his disciples.” through “They should sing especially at Rama's palace gate, where rituals are performed, and in front of the priests.” through “Do not show even a little greed for wealth, for what use is wealth to those who live in hermitages and subsist on fruits and roots?”

Themes: mind and meditation, virtue and right conduct, knowledge and discernment. The passage moves from “If Rama, the descendant of Kakutstha, asks, "Whose boys are you two?", then tell the king, "We are the disciples of Valmiki."” through “Therefore, you two, with joyful minds, should take your seats tomorrow morning and sing the sweet song, accompanied by string instruments and…” through “Then, those two princes, having fixed those wonderful words spoken by the sage in their hearts, passed the night with great eagerness, just as…”

verses.sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथाभूते यज्ञे च परमाद्भुते । सशिष्य आजगामाशु वाल्मीकिर्मुनिपुङ्गवः ।। 7.93.1 ।।
verses.translation: While that supremely wonderful sacrifice was thus going on, Valmiki, the foremost among sages, quickly arrived with his disciples.

verses.sanskrit: स दृष्ट्वा दिव्यसङ्काशं यज्ञमद्भुतदर्शनम् । एकान्ते ऋषिवाटानां चकार उटजाञ्छुभान् ।। 7.93.2 ।।
verses.translation: Having seen the divine-like and wondrous sacrifice, he (Valmiki) constructed auspicious huts in a secluded spot among the hermitages of other sages.

verses.sanskrit: शकटांश्च बहून्पूर्णान्फलमूलैश्च शोभनान् । वाल्मीकिवाटे रुचिरे स्थापयन्नविदूरतः ।। 7.93.3 ।।
verses.translation: He also had many carts, full of excellent fruits and roots, placed not far off in his charming hermitage.

verses.sanskrit: आसीत्सुपूजितो राज्ञा मुनिभिश्च महात्मभिः । वाल्मीकिः सुमहातेजा न्यवसत्परमात्मवान् ।। 7.93.4 ।।
verses.translation: Valmiki, of great splendor and self-controlled, was greatly honored by the king and the great-souled sages, and thus he resided there.

verses.sanskrit: स शिष्यावब्रवीद्धृष्टौ युवां गत्वा समाहितौ । कृत्स्नं रामायणं काव्यं गायेथां परया मुदा ।। 7.93.5 ।।
verses.translation: Valmiki, delighted, instructed his two disciples to go and sing the entire epic poem of Ramayana with utmost concentration and joy.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषिवाटेषु पुण्येषु ब्राह्मणावसथेषु च । रथ्यासु राजमार्गेषु पार्थिवानां गृहेषु च ।। 7.93.6 ।।
verses.translation: They were to sing in sacred hermitages of sages, in the homes of Brahmins, in streets, on royal highways, and in the palaces of kings.

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य भवनद्वारि यत्र कर्म च कुर्वते । ऋत्विजामग्रतश्चैव तत्र गेयं विशेषतः ।। 7.93.7 ।।
verses.translation: They should sing especially at Rama's palace gate, where rituals are performed, and in front of the priests.

verses.sanskrit: इमानि च फलान्यत्र स्वादूनि विविधानि च । जातानि पर्वताग्रेषु चास्वाद्यास्वाद्य गायताम् ।। 7.93.8 ।।
verses.translation: While singing, they should repeatedly taste these various delicious fruits that grow on the mountain peaks.

verses.sanskrit: न यास्यथः श्रमं वत्सौ भक्षयित्वा फलानि वै । मूलानि च सुमृष्टानि नगराद्बहिरास्यथः ।। 7.93.9 ।।
verses.translation: O dear children, you two will not experience fatigue by eating fruits and pure roots, and you will stay outside the city.

verses.sanskrit: यदि शब्दापयेद्रामः श्रवणाय महीपतिः । ऋषीणामुपविष्टानां ततो गेयं प्रवर्तताम् ।। 7.93.10 ।।
verses.translation: If King Rama calls for the sages to listen, then the singing should begin.

verses.sanskrit: दिवसे विंशतिः सर्गा गेया मधुरया गिरा । प्रमाणैर्बहुभिस्तत्र यथोद्दिष्टा मया पुरा ।। 7.93.11 ।।
verses.translation: Twenty cantos should be sung daily with a sweet voice, using the many meters and measures as I have previously instructed.

verses.sanskrit: लोभश्चापि न कर्तव्यः स्वल्पो ऽपि धनकाङ्क्षया । किं धनेनाश्रमस्थानां फलमूलोपजीविना ।। 7.93.12 ।।
verses.translation: Do not show even a little greed for wealth, for what use is wealth to those who live in hermitages and subsist on fruits and roots?

verses.sanskrit: यदि पृच्छेत्स काकुत्स्थो युवां कस्येति दारकौ । आवां वाल्मीकिशिष्यौ स्वो ब्रूतमेवं नराधिपम् ।। 7.93.13 ।।
verses.translation: If Rama, the descendant of Kakutstha, asks, "Whose boys are you two?", then tell the king, "We are the disciples of Valmiki."

verses.sanskrit: इमास्तन्त्रीः सुमधुराः स्थानं वा ऽपूर्वदर्शनम् । मूर्च्छयित्वा सुमधुरं गायतां विगतज्वरौ ।। 7.93.14 ।।
verses.translation: Harmonize these very sweet strings or notes in an unprecedented manner, and sing very sweetly, free from any anxiety.

verses.sanskrit: आदिप्रभृति गेयं स्यान्न चावज्ञाय पार्थिवम् । पिता हि सर्वभूतानां राजा भविति धर्मतः ।। 7.93.15 ।।
verses.translation: The Ramayana should be sung from the beginning, and the king should not be disrespected, for a king is indeed the father of all beings by virtue of dharma.

verses.sanskrit: तद्युवां हृष्टमनसौ श्वः प्रभाते समास्थितौ । गायेथां मधुरं गेयं तन्त्रीलयसमन्वितम् ।। 7.93.16 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, you two, with joyful minds, should take your seats tomorrow morning and sing the sweet song, accompanied by string instruments and rhythm.

verses.sanskrit: इति सन्दिश्य बहुशो मुनिः प्राचेतसस्तदा । वाल्मीकिः परमोदारस्तूष्णीमासीन्महायशाः ।। 7.93.17 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, having repeatedly instructed them, the highly renowned and most noble sage Valmiki, son of Prachetas, then remained silent.

verses.sanskrit: सन्दिष्टौ मुनिना तेन तावुभौ मैथिलीसुतौ । तथैव करवावेति निर्जग्मतुररिन्दमौ ।। 7.93.18 ।।
verses.translation: Having been instructed by that sage, both sons of Maithili, those subduers of enemies, departed, saying, "We shall do just so."

verses.sanskrit: तामद्भुतां तौ हृदये कुमारौ निवेश्य वाणीमृषिभाषितां तदा । समुत्सुकौ तौ सममूषतुर्निशां यथा ऽश्विनौ भार्गवनीतिसंहिताम् ।। 7.93.19 ।।
verses.translation: Then, those two princes, having fixed those wonderful words spoken by the sage in their hearts, passed the night with great eagerness, just as the Ashvins awaited the wisdom of Bhargava. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे त्रिनवतितमः सर्गः ।। 93 ।। Thus ends the ninety-third sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
